decisions.reasoning: Since both Incineroars are identical and speed-tied, using Parting Shot allows me to pivot out safely, debuff the opponent, and gain momentum by bringing in a more favorable matchup.
Since both Incineroars are speed-tied and neither can OHKO the other, Knock Off is the optimal play to remove the opponent's item, providing long-term utility while maintaining momentum.

Calyrex-Ice is slower than Incineroar and will be 2HKOed by Flare Blitz while only 2HKOing back with High Horsepower. Switching to my own Incineroar allows me to Intimidate the opponent and safely pivot or check their physical attackers.
Since both Incineroars resist each other's primary attacks, Parting Shot is the best way to gain momentum, lower the opponent's stats, and bring in a teammate that can better handle the matchup.

Incineroar is at 77% HP and Iron Hands' Drain Punch is a guaranteed 2HKO, meaning staying in leads to a loss of momentum. Switching to Amoonguss provides a safe resistance to Iron Hands' STAB moves and allows for recovery or status pressure.

Iron Hands is a significant threat that can 2HKO Amoonguss with Ice Punch. Spore is the highest value play to neutralize the threat immediately, as Iron Hands is slower and likely to stay in to attack.
Iron Hands is currently asleep, and Spore is the most effective way to keep it neutralized or reset the sleep counter if it wakes up, preventing it from using its threatening Ice Punch.

Iron Hands is a significant threat with Life Orb Ice Punch. Spore is the highest-value play to neutralize it immediately, as it has no immunity to sleep and Amoonguss is bulky enough to survive a hit if it stays in.

Iron Hands is already asleep (SLP). Spore is the best move to keep it incapacitated while I evaluate the board, as it effectively wastes the opponent's turn while they are forced to stay in or switch out a sleeping Pokemon.
The opponent's Iron Hands is already asleep, and Spore will reset the sleep counter, keeping the threat neutralized while I maintain positioning.

Iron Hands is a significant threat with Life Orb Ice Punch, and Spore is the most effective way to neutralize it immediately. Since Amoonguss survives any single hit from Iron Hands, putting it to sleep provides the best utility to pivot or set up later.

Iron Hands is already asleep (as indicated by the HP status), so using Spore again is a waste of a turn. However, since the opponent is asleep and I cannot KO them, I should use Protect to scout their next move or force them to waste their Life Orb recoil if they wake up, while keeping Amoonguss healthy.

Iron Hands is at 56% HP and is using Life Orb, making it vulnerable to being put to sleep. Spore is the highest value play to neutralize the threat and gain momentum, as Iron Hands is likely to stay in to attack.

Iron Hands is already asleep, but Spore is the safest play to maintain sleep pressure if they wake up or switch. Since Iron Hands is currently asleep, this effectively wastes their turn while keeping Amoonguss safe.
Iron Hands is at 42% HP and is a major threat with Life Orb Ice Punch. Spore is the highest-value play to neutralize it completely, as Iron Hands is likely to stay in to fish for a KO on Amoonguss.

Iron Hands is already asleep, but since I cannot KO it and it threatens a 2HKO with Ice Punch, refreshing the sleep status or forcing a switch is the safest play to maintain momentum while keeping Amoonguss healthy.

Iron Hands is already asleep, but since it is the only move that provides utility and the opponent is likely to switch or stay in, Spore is the safest play to maintain control or re-sleep if they wake up.
Iron Hands is already asleep, but Spore is the safest play to ensure it remains incapacitated while I chip it down or pivot, as it is the biggest immediate threat on the field.

Iron Hands is at 28% HP and is a major threat with Life Orb Ice Punch. Spore effectively neutralizes it, allowing me to either finish it off next turn or pivot safely, and it is the highest-value play given the opponent's likely aggressive stance.

Iron Hands is already asleep, but since I have no way to KO it this turn and it is a significant threat with Life Orb, refreshing the sleep status or forcing a switch is the safest play to maintain momentum.

Iron Hands is at 13% HP and likely to stay in to finish off Amoonguss or switch. Spore effectively neutralizes Iron Hands regardless of its move choice, and since it is a low-HP threat, putting it to sleep prevents it from doing any further damage while I decide my next move.

Iron Hands is already asleep, but since it is at 13% HP and I have no move that can reliably KO it this turn without risking a wake-up or a switch, Spore is the safest play to keep it neutralized while I decide on the next phase of the game.
Iron Hands is already asleep, but since I cannot KO it this turn and it is a significant threat, re-applying Spore ensures it stays incapacitated while I maintain board control.

Tornadus-Therian is highly likely to click Hurricane to secure the OHKO on Amoonguss. Protecting allows me to scout the move and potentially waste their turn or force a switch, while keeping Amoonguss alive for a potential Spore later.

Amoonguss is threatened with an OHKO by Hurricane and cannot effectively threaten Tornadus-Therian. Switching to Incineroar provides a safe pivot to resist potential special attacks and scout the opponent's next move.
Flare Blitz is a 2HKO on Tornadus-Therian, and since I am ahead in the exchange, I should press the advantage to secure the KO before it can pivot out or chip me further.

Incineroar is at 9% HP and will be KO'd by almost any move from Tornadus-Therian. Using Flare Blitz provides the highest damage output to weaken the opponent before Incineroar faints, maximizing chip damage for the rest of the team.
Incineroar has fainted, so I must send in a new Pokemon. Calyrex-Ice is the best offensive pivot to threaten Tornadus-Therian with its STAB moves while resisting Hurricane.
Glacial Lance is a guaranteed OHKO on Tornadus-Therian. Since I can secure the KO this turn, I must attack regardless of the speed disadvantage.

Incineroar outspeeds and 2HKOs Calyrex-Ice with Flare Blitz, while I can only 2HKO with High Horsepower. Switching to Amoonguss allows me to tank the incoming Flare Blitz or Knock Off and pivot into a better position.
Amoonguss is at 2% HP and will be OHKOed by Flare Blitz. Switching to Calyrex-Ice preserves the turn and brings in a powerful attacker to threaten the Incineroar.

Calyrex-Ice is at 38% HP and will be KO'd by Flare Blitz or even a combination of moves, while High Horsepower is only a 2HKO. Switching to Amoonguss preserves Calyrex-Ice for a potential late-game cleanup and forces Incineroar to deal with a bulky pivot.
Amoonguss has fainted; I must send in a healthy check to Incineroar. Kingambit resists Incineroar's Knock Off and can threaten back with its STAB moves.

Calyrex-Ice is at 38% and will be KO'd by Flare Blitz, while Incineroar outspeeds. Switching to Kingambit provides a resistance to Dark-type moves and allows me to preserve Calyrex-Ice for a potential late-game cleanup.
